description: k8s maps HTTP 403 (Forbidden) → SecretError::Unavailable via map_kube_error's is_forbidden() arm. vault and doppler both map 403 → SecretError::Backend (permanent). Forbidden typically means a permanent IAM/RBAC misconfiguration that retrying will not fix; mapping it Unavailable incorrectly signals to callers that retry is appropriate. A caller implementing backoff retry on Unavailable will spin indefinitely on a k8s 403 while the same caller would correctly stop retrying on vault/doppler. Fix: map 403 to Backend in k8s, consistent with vault and doppler.
title: HTTP 403 maps to Unavailable in k8s but Backend in vault/doppler
